methods.description: A method to analyze the phylogeny of thylacinid and dasyuromorphian species using a character-taxon matrix. This analysis aims to reconstruct evolutionary relationships and test hypotheses about species' phylogenetic positions.

steps.description: A new matrix was assembled by combining data from previous analyses with the addition of some new character scores for Th. potens and Th. megiriani.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble character-taxon matrix from previous analyses and new data
inputs: new character scores for Th. potens and Th. megiriani

steps.description: The ingroup was restricted to Thylacinidae and characters that were uninformative within the restricted ingroup were excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: restrict matrix to Thylacinidae and exclude uninformative characters

steps.description: Two dasyurids (Barinya wangala and Antechinus flavipes) were employed as outgroup taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: select outgroup taxa for phylogenetic analysis

steps.description: The resulting matrix was subjected to a maximum parsimony analysis in PAUP 4.0b using the following settings: heuristic search; random addition sequence with 500 replicates; and TBR branch-swapping algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform maximum parsimony analysis using PAUP 4.0b

steps.description: The strength of the internal nodes was tested with a bootstrap analysis (1000 bootstrap replicates, heuristic searching with 50 addition sequence replicates).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct bootstrap analysis to test node strength

steps.description: Graphical representation of the common cladistic information of the most parsimonious trees (MPTs) was achieved by a posteriori pruning of labile taxa, i.e., a reduced cladistic consensus tree was produced.
steps.searchable_summary: create reduced cladistic consensus tree by pruning labile taxa
